user_story: As an agent, I want to read the engine version from a single stdout line so I can verify compatibility before running any doc commands.
expected_behavior: Reads version via readEngineVersion() from the engine's own package.json, writes it to stdout as `<semver>\n`, exits 0. Identical whether invoked as `htw -v`, `htw --version`, or `htw version`. No config file is read. [code: bin/htw.mjs:96-99]
hermes_relevance: Hermes calls `npx how-to-work@latest --version` at session start to confirm the pinned/latest engine. Output is a bare semver + trailing newline — trivially parseable. No root or config needed.

user_story: As an agent, I want a machine-readable command listing so I can discover available subcommands without reading source code.
expected_behavior: Writes the HELP constant to stdout, then exits 1 when invoked with NO command, exits 0 with an explicit help flag or the string 'help' (check is `command ? 0 : 1`). Unknown commands write to stderr and exit 1. [code: bin/htw.mjs:91-94, 135-136]
hermes_relevance: Hermes treats exit-1 from bare `htw` as an expected usage guard, not a crash. `htw --help` exits 0 and is parseable. Unknown-command stderr+exit-1 should be surfaced as a fatal error.

user_story: As an agent running in a different working directory than the target repo, I want to aim all operations at a specific repo path without changing my own cwd.
expected_behavior: extractRoot() scans the full argv for `--root <value>` or `--root=<value>` anywhere (not just position 0), removes the pair, returns the remainder as `rest`. Engine-native commands (init/check/grill/serve/index) get the resolved absolute root passed explicitly; doc-kit passthrough (new/render/register/verify/contract) is spawned with cwd:root. Defaults to process.cwd() when omitted. [code: bin/htw.mjs:52-69,101-103,126-129; src/commands/doc-kit.mjs:24]
hermes_relevance: Critical for Hermes driving a specific repo from a shared runtime. Pass `--root /abs/path` before the subcommand so all writes land in the right tree. The flag is consumed before dispatch and never forwarded to a subcommand.

user_story: As an agent scaffolding a new document, I want a well-formed .doc.md source with correct frontmatter so I can author content immediately without boilerplate.
expected_behavior: --kind must be report|working-doc|prd; --slug required (kebab-case [a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*); --title defaults to humanize(slug). PRD output: {prdsDir}/{slug}/index.doc.md; report/working-doc: {sourcesDir}/{slug}.doc.md. die() if output exists. PRDs also scaffold empty state.json (initial stage/status/progressPct/next_action) + empty ledger.jsonl so verify passes. Reports each created file to stdout. [code: src/doc-kit.mjs:1693-1751]
hermes_relevance: Hermes authoring a PRD calls `htw new --kind prd --slug <kebab> --title "..."`. Must slugify titles before passing. --title is optional but recommended so generated frontmatter carries a human title rather than a humanized slug guess.
error_notes: The slug is not validated against the kebab-case pattern [a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*. Slug validation in cmdNew only checks 'if (!slug)' (empty check). An invalid slug like 'INVALID SLUG!' is accepted without error, and a directory named 'INVALID SLUG!' is created on disk. The spec says die() should be called for non-kebab-case slugs. | FIXED P3

user_story: As an agent that has authored or edited a .doc.md source, I want to re-render it to self-contained HTML so the live doc surface updates.
expected_behavior: No args defaults to --all (discovers all .doc.md under sourcesDir + PRD subdirs); --all discovers the same; explicit paths render only those. Validates frontmatter before rendering; die() on any validation error. Output: {prdsDir}/{slug}/index.html (PRDs) or {plansDir}/{slug}.html (reports/working-docs) via outputPathFor(). Reports `rendered <src> -> <out>`. [code: src/doc-kit.mjs:1754-1768]
hermes_relevance: After editing a .doc.md, Hermes calls `htw render <path/to/source.doc.md>` to update HTML. Full refresh: `htw render --all`. The `rendered <src> -> <out>` lines confirm which files changed.
error_notes: When an absolute path is passed as an explicit render argument, the engine crashes with an unhandled Node.js exception (full stack trace to stderr) rather than a clean error message. Root cause: parseSource() uses path.join(ROOT, srcPath) which does not treat an absolute srcPath as absolute — instead it prepends ROOT to the entire string, doubling the path. The spec says 'explicit paths render only those' without restricting to relative paths, and agents naturally use absolute paths. | FIXED P3

user_story: As an agent that has rendered a doc, I want to add/update its catalog entry so it appears in the index dashboard and is discoverable.
expected_behavior: When config.doc.catalogPath ends in .json (public default docs/catalog.json), upserts a plain JSON object into the array, sorted by id for deterministic diffs. When .ts (Homebase legacy src/modules/docs/catalog.ts), in-place splice replaces/appends the TS entry. Redirected docs (frontmatter redirectTo) skipped with a notice. Reports `added <slug>`/`updated <slug>`. Without --all a source path is required. [code: src/doc-kit.mjs:1825-1857]
hermes_relevance: Hermes calls `htw register --all` (or per-slug) after render to keep the catalog current. JSON catalog is the Hermes-friendly default — a plain array parseable with JSON.parse. The TS path is Homebase-only.

user_story: As an agent that has updated the catalog, I want to rebuild the docs index page so a human can navigate all docs by lifecycle stage from one HTML entry point.
expected_behavior: Reads docs/catalog.json (error if absent or not a JSON array). Buckets entries into Idea/Scoping/Active/Done/Archive via LIFECYCLE_SECTION plus an 'Other' bucket for unrecognized values. Renders each as a card with a segmented stage bar (matching doc theme). Writes self-contained docs/index.html. Reports `wrote docs/index.html (<N> docs)`. Exits 1 if catalog missing or not an array. [code: src/commands/index.mjs:165-263]
hermes_relevance: Hermes calls `htw index` after any `htw register` that touches the catalog. Idempotent, side-effect-free beyond writing one file. The stdout confirmation + exit code are the only machine-readable outputs.

user_story: As an agent, I want to validate that every doc source has valid frontmatter, a matching rendered HTML, a catalog entry with correct href, and (PRDs) preserved state/ledger files, so I can gate a deploy or PR on doc health.
expected_behavior: Defaults to --all with no args. Per-source PASS/FAIL lines with specific reasons. Validates theme.css (must start at :root after comment strip, balanced braces). JSON catalog: checks id exists + entry.href matches expected route; TS catalog: regex on raw file. Exits 1 if any source or theme fails; exits 0 with `verify: OK (N docs)` otherwise. [code: src/doc-kit.mjs:1860-1990]
hermes_relevance: Hermes runs `htw verify --all` after render+register for a machine-checkable gate. `PASS <src>`/`FAIL <src>` is parseable (split on first space). The `verify: OK`/`verify: FAILED` summary is the easiest single-line check; exit 1 fails a step without parsing stdout.

user_story: As an agent starting to author docs in an unfamiliar repo, I want a self-contained reference for all required frontmatter, block types, and authoring rules so I produce valid sources without reading source code.
expected_behavior: cmdContract() prints a multi-section text contract to stdout via console.log(): doc kinds, required/optional frontmatter, stage bar, custom tabs (@tab), all block types (:::callout/rows/cards/decisions/questions/progress/ledger/resources/html), authoring rules. Output is identical regardless of repo config (describes engine contract, not repo state). No flags, no side effects, exits 0. [code: src/doc-kit.mjs:1627-1691]
hermes_relevance: Hermes calls `htw contract` once at the start of a doc-authoring task and treats stdout as a system-prompt supplement. Plain text — read as a rules document, not structured data.

user_story: As an agent running a local docs review session, I want a zero-dependency HTTP server serving rendered docs so a human can open them in a browser with no framework or build step.
expected_behavior: Binds 127.0.0.1 by default on port 8765 (or config.serve.port). Serves only the /docs/* subtree (GET/HEAD); all other paths return plain-text 404. Directory requests resolve to index.html. Path-traversal guard: resolved file must be inside {root}/docs. Correct MIME from extension; Cache-Control: no-store on every response. Calls configureStore({dataDir: root/data}). Runs forever (never resolves); Ctrl-C to stop. [code: src/commands/serve.mjs:63-132]
hermes_relevance: Hermes starts `htw serve` (background) to get a live doc server with no custom HTTP layer. For the grill loop it must add --answer-gate; without onAnswer it is mode-2 (local standalone), so Hermes polls /result or uses `htw grill ask` as a blocking subprocess.
error_notes: The spec says 'all other paths return plain-text 404' for non-/docs/* paths. However, the root path '/' returns HTTP 200 with the full docs/index.html content. serve.mjs line 66 explicitly remaps '/', '/docs', and '/docs/' to '/docs/index.html' before the /docs-subtree guard runs. The spec contract is violated: '/' is not restricted to the /docs subtree and does not return 404. | DECISION P3: '/' intentionally serves docs/index.html (friendly root alias; traversal guard confines to docs/). Expected behavior updated.

user_story: As an agent enabling the live grill loop, I want the answer-gate API mounted on the same origin as the docs so the doc's Submit button can POST answers and the polling client can retrieve them.
expected_behavior: Mounts /api/hwq/* same-origin via createHwqHandler({prefix:'/api/hwq', docsDir}) with onAnswer OMITTED (undefined) — copy/submit/poll mode only, no delivery callback, retry loop does NOT start. Store persists to {root}/data/hwq.json. All seven HWQ routes available: POST /open, GET /status, POST /submit, GET /result, POST /close, POST /retry, GET /docrev. Without --answer-gate those routes are absent and the doc's Submit fails silently. [code: src/commands/serve.mjs:96-108; server/hwq-server.mjs:82-120]
hermes_relevance: Hermes must start `htw serve --answer-gate` before `htw grill ask` (grill ask POSTs /open and polls /result). A private Hermes host can instead mount programmatically WITH an onAnswer callback; the public `htw serve --answer-gate` deliberately omits onAnswer — the delivery seam ships only in the private host.

user_story: As an agent or operator needing docs accessible on a network (Tailscale/LAN), I want to bind the server on a non-loopback address with an explicit port.
expected_behavior: --port overrides config.serve.port (default 8765); --host sets the bind address (default 127.0.0.1). If --host resolves to a non-loopback address (anything outside 127.0.0.1, ::1, localhost, 0:0:0:0:0:0:0:1) and --allow-external is NOT present, the server refuses to start with exit 1 + a clear error to stderr. With --allow-external, any host is accepted. The guard is a synchronous pre-flight, not a per-request check. [code: src/commands/serve.mjs:28,91-101]
hermes_relevance: Hermes driving a remote doc review (Homebase on a Tailscale node) must pass `--host 0.0.0.0 --allow-external`. Without --allow-external the safety guard exits 1, surfaced as a fatal server-start failure.

user_story: As an agent that authored grill cards and needs the human to answer before proceeding, I want a blocking gate that lights up the doc in the browser and waits for the human to submit answers, then delivers them on stdout.
expected_behavior: POSTs {docKey,prompt} to <base>/open (doc shows the asking-banner, CSS 'asking'). Derives docKey as /docs/prds/<slug>/ from --doc (or literal --key). Polls <base>/result?key=<docKey>&since=<openAt> every --poll ms (default 1500) until j.submission arrives or --timeout ms elapses (default 30min). Success: emits the submission JSON between ===HWQ-ANSWERS-BEGIN===/===HWQ-ANSWERS-END=== on stdout (+ delivery block if present), exits 0. Timeout: best-effort POST /close, exit 2. Open failure (not a connection error): exit 3. Bad subcommand: exit 64. Connection errors mid-poll are swallowed (server-restart tolerance). [code: src/commands/grill.mjs:171-261]
hermes_relevance: Primary Hermes<->human handoff. Hermes blocks on `htw grill ask --doc <slug>`, parses the submission JSON from stdout as {doc,docKey,total,answers:[{id,title,decision,comment}],payload,submittedAt}. Exit 0=answers, 2=timeout, 3=open failed, 64=usage. Hermes surfaces exit 2 as 'no answers received in time'.
error_notes: The core blocking behavior works: POSTs to /open with docKey='/docs/prds/test-prd/', polls /result, exits 2 on timeout, calls /close as cleanup. However the timeout display in stderr always shows '0m' for any timeout under 1 minute (Math.round(timeoutMs / 60000) floors sub-minute values to 0). For 5000ms timeout: 'timeout 0m'. For the 30-minute default this shows correctly as '30m', but for short test/dev timeouts the display is meaningless. | FIXED P3

user_story: As an agent that wants to light up the doc's question section without blocking my execution thread, I want to open the ask asynchronously and check for answers later.
expected_behavior: After a successful POST /open, immediately writes {ok:true, docKey, openAt, mode:'open-only'} as JSON to stdout and exits 0. Does NOT start the polling loop. --open-only and --background are aliases of --no-wait. [code: src/commands/grill.mjs:183-185,224-228]
hermes_relevance: Hermes fires `htw grill ask --doc <slug> --no-wait` to open a gate, captures the JSON (records openAt), then later GETs <base>/result?key=<docKey>&since=<openAt> directly — no blocking subprocess. Correct for multi-step flows that queue multiple asks before blocking on any.

user_story: As an agent connecting to a non-default answer-gate (e.g. Homebase's Vite middleware at a different URL), I want to specify the full answer-gate base URL explicitly.
expected_behavior: resolveBase() checks --base first, then config.answerGate.base. Relative values (no https?:// prefix) are prepended with config.devUrlBase (default http://127.0.0.1:8765) to form an absolute URL. Trailing slashes stripped. Routes appended as <base>/open, <base>/result, <base>/close. [code: src/commands/grill.mjs:64-72]
hermes_relevance: When Homebase hosts the gate (port 3141, /api/hwq), Hermes passes `--base http://127.0.0.1:3141/api/hwq` — the primary Homebase integration path. When config.answerGate.base is already correct, --base can be omitted.

user_story: As an agent in a time-sensitive flow, I want to control how often I check for answers and how long I wait before giving up, to tune urgent vs. relaxed reviews.
expected_behavior: --poll sets the interval between /result polls (default 1500ms). --timeout sets the total wait budget (default 1,800,000ms = 30min). After the deadline, /close is called and exit code 2 is returned with `timed out with no submission`. [code: src/commands/grill.mjs:187-188,231-259]
hermes_relevance: Hermes tunes --timeout to context (e.g. 300000 = 5min for quick decisions, 86400000 = 24h for async reviews). --poll 3000 reduces request noise. Both optional with sensible defaults.
error_notes: Functional behavior correct: --poll 500 --timeout 3000 exits 2 after ~3076ms with 'timed out with no submission'. However the timeout display has the same 'Math.round(timeoutMs / 60000)' floor-to-zero issue as F013: stderr shows 'timeout 0m' for a 3-second timeout. For sub-minute timeout values, the display is uninformative. | FIXED P3

user_story: As an agent in an environment where the answer-gate server is not running, I want the CLI to gracefully degrade to a stdin-based flow so the human (or I) can still provide answers via shorthand.
expected_behavior: When --stdin-fallback is set and the initial /open fails with a connection-class error (ECONNREFUSED, ENOTFOUND, ECONNRESET, EHOSTUNREACH, ETIMEDOUT, UND_ERR_SOCKET, UND_ERR_CONNECT_TIMEOUT, or a 'fetch failed' TypeError), instead of exit 3 it prints the open questions extracted from the rendered HTML (via data-qid/data-qtitle regex) to stderr, then reads shorthand from stdin. Shorthand: `<n>r [— note]` => approve; `<n> <answer>` => custom; tokens split on `|` or newlines. On EOF emits the same ===HWQ-ANSWERS-BEGIN===/END=== output with source:'stdin-fallback'. Does NOT trigger on HTTP 4xx/5xx. Exits 0. [code: src/commands/grill.mjs:37-62,74-95,109-169,207-213]
hermes_relevance: Hermes can pipe shorthand directly: `echo '1r | 2 override text' | htw grill ask --doc <slug> --stdin-fallback` — a fully offline answer path for testing, CI without a mounted gate, or scripted approvals. The emitted JSON shape is identical to the online path, so downstream parsing is the same.

feature: Default walnut theme — light-mode surface ramp tokens
user_story: As an agent rendering a doc in a bare repo, I want every surface (background, ink, borders, cards, chips) to look polished without configuration so docs are immediately presentable.
expected_behavior: The :root block declares eight surface tokens: --bg #f7f4ee (parchment), --fg #1f1d1a (warm near-black ink), --muted #6f685e, --line #ddd4c6 (hairlines), --panel #fffaf2 (card/callout/tab fill), --soft #eee5d7 (chips/code/insets), --accent #6f4d2f (walnut — links/focus/active), --on-accent #fdfaf4. All HTML components reference these tokens — no hardcoded hex in layout rules — so the whole surface is driven by this one :root block. [code: templates/theme.css:28-68]
hermes_relevance: Theme is inlined at render time by themeCss(); no runtime CSS resolution. `htw render` output HTML is self-contained (theme inlined verbatim), renderable in any browser/static host with no network dependency.
error_notes: All 8 surface tokens are correctly declared in :root and the CSS is inlined (no external stylesheet links). However, `templates/theme.css` line 742 inside `.qcard[data-state="answered"]` uses a hardcoded `rgba(31, 29, 26, 0.06)` for the ambient shadow layer, bypassing the token system. The spec states 'All HTML components reference these tokens — no hardcoded hex in layout rules.' While the spec says 'hex' specifically, this hardcoded rgba is the light-mode ink color (--fg) at 6% opacity and does not adapt in dark mode. The dark-mode --shadow-border token uses `rgba(0,0,0,0.34)` for ambient depth; this answered-card rule stays on the light-mode value. Fix: replace with `color-mix(in oklab, var(--fg) 6%, transparent)` or reference `var(--shadow-border)` ambient layers. | FIXED P3

feature: Default walnut theme — semantic color tokens
user_story: As an agent writing a grill answer or decision table, I want Decided/Open/Risk statuses to carry consistent color meaning (green/amber/red) so humans parse state at a glance.
expected_behavior: Three semantic tokens alongside the surface ramp: --green #1f6b4a (decided/done/approve), --amber #8a5a12 (open/pending/caution), --red #9f2f2f (risk/blocker/disapprove). Applied as inline styles on .status/.warn, aria-pressed fill on .qapprove/.qreject, and .qcopy.copied/.qsubmit.done states. Tone is the engine's responsibility — agents write semantic .doc.md, never hardcode colors. [code: templates/theme.css:41-43]
hermes_relevance: Hermes never emits raw color CSS. Grill answers go via the gate/CLI; tone is applied by the renderer from decision/question semantics. `htw verify` output uses text state strings (Decided/Open), not color codes.

feature: Default walnut theme — shape and shadow-as-border
user_story: As a doc viewer on any device, I want cards to feel elevated with depth cues rather than flat borders, and corners to stay sharp to match the editorial house style.
expected_behavior: --radius is 0 (sharp rectangles, house style). --shadow-border is a layered transparent shadow: a 0 0 0 1px hairline ring at var(--line) plus two ambient depth layers, applied via box-shadow on .card/.qcard. --shadow-border-hover adds a walnut ring (color-mix accent 32% + line) and heavier lift, paired with transform: translateY(-1px) on :hover. transition is enumerated (box-shadow 0.18s ease, transform 0.18s ease) — never `transition: all`. [code: templates/theme.css:49-57,82-88,285-298,408-425]
hermes_relevance: Shadow tokens are CSS-only; Hermes does not inspect shadow values. A re-skin via themeTokens can override --radius (e.g. 8px) to round every card, tab, chip, code block, and button at once.

feature: Dark mode — prefers-color-scheme: dark token override
user_story: As a human reading a rendered doc at night on any browser/OS, I want the doc to flip to a dark editorial palette automatically with no per-doc configuration.
expected_behavior: A @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) block right after :root overrides all eight surface tokens, the three semantic tokens, and both shadow-border variants. Dark values: --bg #1a1714, --fg #ece6dc, --muted #9a9082, --line #3a342d, --panel #221e1a, --soft #2b2620, --accent #c79a6e (warm gold), --on-accent #1a1714, --green #7fce9f, --amber #dcab5e, --red #e89090. Dark shadows use rgba(0,0,0,...). color-scheme: light dark on :root signals form-control rendering. [code: templates/theme.css:29,69-88]
hermes_relevance: Entirely CSS @media — no CLI flag, no build step, no Hermes action. A themeTokens patch overriding :root applies in both modes unless it ships its own @media block; a themeFile full-replace must declare its own dark mode or the doc has none.

feature: Self-hosted fonts — Space Grotesk + Public Sans + Spline Sans Mono as embedded data URIs
user_story: As an agent/human rendering a doc on a machine with no internet (airgapped CI, local dev, Tailscale-only server), I want all typefaces to load instantly with no FOUT and no external requests.
expected_behavior: Custom props: --font-display ('Space Grotesk','Public Sans',ui-sans-serif,...) for h1/h2/h3 + SVG titles; --font-body ('Public Sans',...) for body + components; --font-mono ('Spline Sans Mono',ui-monospace,...) for code/pre. Two @font-face blocks embed Public Sans (weight 100-900, display swap) and Space Grotesk (weight 300-700) as data:font/woff2;base64 URIs. Raw WOFF2 subsets live in templates/fonts/, re-embedded by `node templates/build-fonts.mjs`. Spline Sans Mono has no @font-face — falls back to system mono. [code: templates/theme.css:59-65,949-966]
hermes_relevance: Rendered HTML is fully self-contained — no CDN, no font preload in CI. A themeFile full-replace omitting @font-face loses the bundled faces; a themeTokens patch cannot override @font-face (only :root tokens), so fonts are always bundled from the base theme under tier-2 override.

user_story: As a human reading a rendered doc on macOS/iOS, I want type to appear crisp and anti-aliased rather than sub-pixel chunky, matching a premium editorial feel.
expected_behavior: body { -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; } applied globally on body — every component inherits. No per-element override needed. [code: templates/theme.css:99-101]
hermes_relevance: CSS on the generated body element; always present in output regardless of theme-override tier. A themeFile full-replace must include its own smoothing declarations if desired.

user_story: As a human reading a heading or paragraph, I want headings to never leave a lone word dangling on the last line, and body copy to break aesthetically.
expected_behavior: h1/h2/h3 declare text-wrap: balance; .lede (subtitle), plain p, and li declare text-wrap: pretty. Browser-native CSS in the base theme; no JS polyfill or author action. [code: templates/theme.css:136,145,153,159,162-164]
hermes_relevance: Applied passively at render time; no Hermes-specific consideration. A themeFile full-replace should preserve these for prose quality.

user_story: As a human scanning a ledger timeline, chip IDs, or stage counter, I want digits to align in columns so I can visually diff numbers across rows.
expected_behavior: font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums applied to .chip, .qid, .qcount ('X of Y answered'), .stagestep ('2/7'), ledger .event .small (timestamp/who), and code (inline). Prose uses proportional spacing. [code: templates/theme.css:176,218,387,443,486,766]
hermes_relevance: Passive CSS. Ledger/stage data emitted via `htw render` gets tabular alignment for free. No action required.

user_story: As a keyboard user navigating tabs, grill buttons, the back link, or answer controls, I want a clear visible focus indicator so I can track my position.
expected_behavior: Every interactive element has a :focus-visible rule with outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 2px — .back, a, .tab, .qclear, .qcopy, .qsubmit, .qbtn. The prior regression (outline:none; box-shadow:none on .tab) is fixed. Accent-colored (walnut light / gold dark), only on keyboard focus (focus-visible). 40px min hit area on .tab and .qbtn. [code: templates/theme.css:125-128,169-173,231,257-260,535-540,679,699-701]
hermes_relevance: Human a11y concern, but a Hermes-driven Playwright flow sees these indicators when tabbing through interactive elements; the 40px min hit area also aids automated tap/click reliability.

user_story: As a human landing on a freshly rendered doc, I want cards to enter in a staggered cascade (not all at once) so the page feels intentional.
expected_behavior: @keyframes docCardIn: from {opacity:0; translateY(8px); blur(2px)} to {opacity:1; translateY(0); blur(0)}. Applied to .grid>.card, .qstack>.qcard, section.card with animation 0.5s cubic-bezier(0.2,0.7,0.2,1) backwards. Stagger by nth-child: 0/70/140/210/280/350ms, (n+7)=420ms. `backwards` fill holds opacity:0 during delay (no flash). Under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, animation is none. [code: templates/theme.css:856-866,878-924]
hermes_relevance: CSS-only; does not affect HTML structure or CLI output. Hermes renders need not wait for animations. In headless screenshot diffs (where prefers-reduced-motion is often off), account for the 420ms max stagger before capture.

user_story: As a human clicking between tabs in a PRD/progress doc, I want the panel content to fade in smoothly rather than appear instantly.
expected_behavior: @keyframes docPanelIn: from {opacity:0} to {opacity:1}. Applied to .panel.active via animation 0.28s ease both. Suppressed under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce. Tab state is driven by inline TAB_SCRIPT JS that toggles .active. [code: templates/theme.css:266-268,868-875,912-924; src/doc-kit.mjs:130-149]
hermes_relevance: A Hermes-driven headless browser clicking tabs triggers this animation; in automated capture, a ~280ms settle is enough before screenshotting the active panel.

feature: Polish: card hover lift + will-change discipline
user_story: As a human hovering a card or question card, I want a subtle lift (shadow intensifies, card rises 1px) confirming interactivity, without expensive layout thrash.
expected_behavior: .card:hover/.qcard:hover apply box-shadow var(--shadow-border-hover) + transform translateY(-1px). Transitions are enumerated (box-shadow 0.18s ease, transform 0.18s ease) — never `transition: all`. will-change: transform is set ONLY on .qbtn (paint-promotion justified); .card/.qcard do not use will-change. Under prefers-reduced-motion, .card/.qcard transition: none (hover lift disabled). [code: templates/theme.css:285-298,408-425,688,921-923]
hermes_relevance: Human UX only; no Hermes action. The enumerated-transitions rule matters when an agent writes a themeFile full-replace — follow the same pattern to avoid perf regressions.

feature: Three delivery modes: none/copy-only, local standalone, custom onAnswer
user_story: As an agent or human driving the grill loop, I need to know which delivery mode the gate runs in so I know whether answers auto-deliver, require polling, or must be copy/pasted.
expected_behavior: Mode is fixed at server-construction by whether onAnswer is passed to createHwqHandler. (1) copy-only: no server; the doc's Copy button emits shorthand a human pastes; no /api/hwq routes. (2) local standalone: `htw serve --answer-gate` with onAnswer undefined — /submit persists to data/hwq.json, `htw grill ask` polls /result, no callback fires. (3) custom onAnswer: createHwqHandler({onAnswer}) — on every /submit the callback fires immediately AND a 5s setInterval retry scan re-delivers on failure (the Hermes path). `deliver` is armed only when typeof onAnswer === 'function'; the timer is .unref()-ed. The public package ships onAnswer=undefined. [code: server/hwq-server.mjs:82-121; src/commands/serve.mjs:107-108]
hermes_relevance: onAnswer IS the Hermes seam. A private Hermes host wires its own onAnswer so answers push to the thread immediately on Submit (no polling). Verify: the public package never wires onAnswer; the delivery infra (backoff, store tracking) exists only for a Hermes-class host to supply.

feature: POST /open — register an ask and light up the doc
user_story: As an agent running `htw grill ask`, I POST /open so the grill-card HTML transitions to its lit-up (answerable) state and I receive askId + openAt to anchor my /result poll.
expected_behavior: Requires docKey in the JSON body; 400 {ok:false, error:'docKey is required'} if missing. On success writes a new HwqAsk to the store (randomUUID askId, openAt:Date.now(), submission:null, delivery:null), overwriting any previous ask for the same docKey. Returns 200 {ok:true, askId, openAt}. The doc's status-poll sees waiting:true on its next GET /status and renders the answer UI. [code: server/hwq-server.mjs:129-135; server/hwq-store.mjs:101-114]
hermes_relevance: Agents call /open before blocking. The returned openAt is the `since` parameter for all subsequent /result polls so the agent never sees a stale submission from a prior session for the same docKey.

feature: GET /status — inspect the current ask state
user_story: As an agent or the doc's JS polling client, I GET /status?key=<docKey> to learn whether the gate is waiting for a human answer and to retrieve delivery progress.
expected_behavior: Reads the ask for ?key. Returns 200 {ok, waiting, askId, openAt, prompt, hasSubmission, submittedAt, delivery}. waiting is true iff ask.openAt is non-null. Returns the same shape with null/false when no ask exists. NEVER 404 for a missing key — always 200. [code: server/hwq-server.mjs:136-151; server/hwq-store.mjs:117-119]
hermes_relevance: Hermes polls /status to check delivery without re-opening the poll loop. The delivery field exposes status (pending/delivered/error), attempts, lastAttemptAt, deliveredAt, error — the full delivery audit trail.

feature: POST /submit — persist answers and trigger optional delivery
user_story: As the in-doc 'Submit to agent' button (or a test harness), I POST /submit with the structured answer payload so the agent's poll loop unblocks and, if a callback is wired, the answers push immediately.
expected_behavior: Requires docKey; 400 if missing. Writes the submission {doc, docKey, total, answers:[{id,title,decision,comment}], payload, submittedAt:Date.now()}. Sets openAt=null (closes/de-lights) and delivery=null (resets). If onAnswer is wired, calls deliverAsk immediately; response includes delivery from that attempt. Returns 200 {ok, submittedAt, delivery} — delivery is null with no callback. normalizeAnswers enforces the {id,title,decision,comment} shape. [code: server/hwq-server.mjs:62-74,152-168; server/hwq-store.mjs:123-145]
hermes_relevance: Where the Hermes push happens. With onAnswer wired, answers reach Hermes in the same HTTP round-trip as the Submit press (no polling lag). delivery.result carries whatever onAnswer(ask) returns, letting the client confirm receipt.

feature: GET /result — blocking poll endpoint for htw grill ask
user_story: As an agent running `htw grill ask`, I poll /result?key=<docKey>&since=<openAt> to detect when a human has answered so I can unblock and process the submission.
expected_behavior: Returns 200 {ok:true, pending:true} while no qualifying submission exists; 200 {ok:true, pending:false, submission, delivery} once ask.submission.submittedAt > since. The since filter ensures the client unblocks only on submissions opened after the current ask (prevents stale prior-session answers from resolving immediately). [code: server/hwq-server.mjs:170-178; src/commands/grill.mjs:231-248]
hermes_relevance: With a custom onAnswer, /result polling is largely redundant (push delivery). Mode-2 (local standalone) relies entirely on polling /result. The since-gating is critical for multi-turn correctness — an agent must not unblock on a prior-session submission.

user_story: As the grill-ask client timing out, or a human cancelling a stale question, I POST /close so the doc reverts to its neutral state and future /status shows waiting:false.
expected_behavior: Sets ask.openAt=null for the given docKey. No error if docKey is unknown (silent no-op). Returns 200 {ok:true}. `htw grill ask` calls /close as best-effort cleanup after its poll timeout before returning exit 2. [code: server/hwq-server.mjs:198-203; server/hwq-store.mjs:217-223; src/commands/grill.mjs:251-258]
hermes_relevance: Hermes calls /close when cancelling a grill session (user aborts mid-run). Failure to close leaves the doc lit up until the next /open overwrites the state.

feature: POST /retry — manually trigger the delivery retry scan
user_story: As an agent/operator who suspects a delivery callback is stuck in error, I POST /retry to kick the pending-delivery scan immediately rather than waiting for the next automatic interval.
expected_behavior: Calls retryPending() directly. With no onAnswer wired, retryPending returns immediately (deliver-null guard). With onAnswer wired, runs listPendingDeliveryAsks() and calls deliverAsk on each passing shouldRetry. Returns 200 {ok:true} always. Does not expose which asks were retried or their outcome. Protected by a scanInFlight mutex so concurrent /retry calls don't stack. [code: server/hwq-server.mjs:97-115,179-181]
hermes_relevance: Hermes can call /retry after observing a delivery error in /status to force immediate re-delivery without waiting for the backoff interval.

feature: GET /docrev — file mtime for doc hot-reload
user_story: As the in-doc JS polling client, I call /docrev?path=<docPath> to detect when the rendered HTML changed on disk so I can reload and pick up re-rendered question cards.
expected_behavior: Resolves ?path relative to docsRoot (stripping a leading /docs/ prefix). Path-traversal guard: resolved path must be inside docsRoot or equal it, else 403 {ok:false, error:'Forbidden'}. Directories resolve to index.html inside. Returns 200 {ok:true, mtime:<mtimeMs>}; returns mtime:0 for missing files (not a 404). [code: server/hwq-server.mjs:183-196]
hermes_relevance: Low direct Hermes relevance — consumed by the rendered doc's browser JS. When Hermes re-runs render, the doc client uses /docrev to reload without a full server restart.

user_story: As a security-conscious operator, I need the answer-gate to refuse to bind to a non-loopback address by default so grill-card answers cannot be submitted from outside the local machine.
expected_behavior: startHwqServer rejects (never starts) if host is not in {127.0.0.1, ::1, localhost, 0:0:0:0:0:0:0:1} and allowExternal is not set — a synchronous pre-flight, not a per-request header check. `htw serve` has the same guard (stderr + exit 1 without binding). Both accept --allow-external / allowExternal:true to override. Default host is 127.0.0.1 in CLI and programmatic API. [code: server/hwq-server.mjs:41-45,216-220; src/commands/serve.mjs:28,91-99]
hermes_relevance: A Hermes host starting the gate via startHwqServer must use 127.0.0.1 or explicitly pass allowExternal:true. Binding to 0.0.0.0 yields a rejected Promise, not a running server. Loopback default ensures the gate is never inadvertently reachable from Tailscale/internet without explicit opt-in.

user_story: As the gate server surviving a restart or crash, I need ask state (open questions, submissions, delivery progress) persisted to disk so the grill loop resumes without loss.
expected_behavior: All ops read/write {dataDir}/hwq.json (default {cwd}/data/hwq.json). configureStore({dataDir}) re-points the location — `htw serve` calls it with {root}/data so the store lives in the consuming repo. Keyed by docKey. normalizeAsk coerces persisted records to the current generic shape (strips legacy hermes-typed fields). readState returns {} on missing file or parse error — never throws. Writes mkdir({recursive:true}). No in-memory cache: every op re-reads and re-writes the JSON. [code: server/hwq-store.mjs:56-98; src/commands/serve.mjs:104]
hermes_relevance: A private Hermes host should call configureStore (or pass dataDir to createHwqHandler) to place hwq.json inside the target repo, not the Hermes process CWD — otherwise a restart could relocate the file. A submitted answer survives a server restart: the next /result returns the stored submission, and the grill-ask poll loop retries on any fetch error.

feature: onAnswer delivery loop — exponential backoff with audit trail
user_story: As an agent whose onAnswer delivery failed (e.g. transient network error posting to the Hermes thread), I need automatic retries with backoff so the answer reaches me without manual intervention.
expected_behavior: After a failed deliverAsk, store records delivery.status='error', increments attempts, sets error. The 5s setInterval scan calls shouldRetry: backoff = min(60000, max(2000, 1000 * 2^min(attempts,6))) ms since lastAttemptAt (saturates at 60s after 6 failures). On success: status='delivered', deliveredAt set, result holds the onAnswer return value. listPendingDeliveryAsks returns all asks with a submission and delivery.status !== 'delivered' — failed deliveries stay queued indefinitely (no give-up limit). scanInFlight mutex prevents concurrent scans. DELIVERY_SCAN_INTERVAL_MS=5000, MAX_DELIVERY_BACKOFF_MS=60000. [code: server/hwq-server.mjs:39-40,87-120,170-176; server/hwq-store.mjs:153-215]
hermes_relevance: Core reliability mechanism for Hermes. Hermes's onAnswer (async, posts to the thread API) self-heals transient failures (thread not yet ready, rate limit) without re-prompting. Hermes reads delivery.status from /result to confirm end-to-end delivery, not just local submission.

feature: HWQ-ANSWERS contract — stdout markers and payload shape
user_story: As a Hermes agent or Claude Code session reading `htw grill ask` output, I parse the stdout between the marker lines to extract the structured submission and act on the human's decisions.
expected_behavior:
Stdout is `
===HWQ-ANSWERS-BEGIN===
` + 2-space-indent JSON of the submission + `
===HWQ-ANSWERS-END===
`. Submission shape: {doc:string, docKey:string, total:number, answers:[{id,title,decision,comment}], payload:string, submittedAt:number}. Optional sections AFTER the END marker: `----- paste-ready payload -----` if payload non-empty, and `----- delivery -----` if j.delivery present. All non-answer output (progress, errors) goes to stderr only. answers[].decision is the primary signal: 'approve'=accept, 'custom'/'disapprove'=override. [code: src/commands/grill.mjs:136-143,237-243; server/hwq-server.mjs:62-74]
hermes_relevance: The machine-readable output contract Hermes parses. Markers are unambiguous delimiters surviving stdout pollution from other htw output. Hermes extracts the JSON between markers; sections after END are human-oriented and ignored.
error_notes: Stdout/stderr separation works correctly: ===HWQ-ANSWERS-BEGIN===/===HWQ-ANSWERS-END=== markers on their own lines, JSON between, paste-ready payload section after END, all status messages to stderr. --no-wait/--background/--open-only all produce {ok:true, docKey, openAt, mode:'open-only'} on stdout and exit 0. stdin-fallback produces same markers with source:'stdin-fallback'. One UX issue: the timeout display in the 'waiting for submission' stderr message uses `Math.round(timeoutMs / 60000)` which produces 'timeout 0m' for any --timeout under 30000ms (e.g. --timeout 8000 → '0m', --timeout 1500 → '0m'). The default 30min shows '30m' correctly. This is confirmed from source: grill.mjs:222 `Math.round(timeoutMs / 60000) + 'm'`. For short agent timeouts (e.g. 5min = 300000ms → '5m') this resolves correctly, but sub-minute debug timeouts show the misleading '0m'. | FIXED P3

feature: Six-level config probe with deep merge
user_story: As an agent running htw in any repo, I want the engine to automatically find and merge all config layers so I never assemble config myself or know the exact file layout.
expected_behavior: loadConfig(root) probes six sources in fixed precedence: (1) BUILTIN_DEFAULTS, (2) {PACKAGE_ROOT}/config/defaults.json, (3) {root}/.claude/skill-config/doc/config.json, (4) {root}/.agents/skill-config/doc/config.json, (5) {root}/.claude/skill-config/workflow/config.json, (6) {root}/.agents/skill-config/workflow/config.json (CANONICAL, highest). Deep-merged: plain objects merge key-by-key; scalars/arrays/null replace wholesale (a higher null DOES override). Missing files silently skipped (readJson returns null). Always includes root + packageRoot. [code: src/config.mjs:57-99]
hermes_relevance: Every command calls loadConfig(process.cwd()) at startup. Hermes writes per-repo overrides to .agents/skill-config/workflow/config.json (source 6, wins all). Unknown keys are inert — Hermes can add agent-guidance fields without breaking anything. Resolution is synchronous at configure() time (no race).

user_story: As a developer with the Homebase repo and no htw config, I want htw to produce byte-identical output to before config support, so upgrading the engine never silently changes rendered docs.
expected_behavior: Every BUILTIN_DEFAULTS value equals the previous Homebase hardcode: overlayDir='.agents/skill-config/doc', catalogPath='src/modules/docs/catalog.ts', sourcesDir='docs/sources', prdsDir='docs/prds', plansDir='docs/plans', docsIndexRoute='/docs', stagesPath=null, themeFile=null, answerGate.base='/api/hwq', answerGate.mode='none', brandName='Docs'. A repo with zero config renders identically to the prior engine version. [code: src/config.mjs:33-48]

feature: Bundled defaults diverge from BUILTIN_DEFAULTS on two keys
user_story: As a new user running htw init in a fresh repo, I want the bundled public defaults to reflect the generic (non-Homebase) layout so my repo does not inherit Homebase-specific paths.
expected_behavior: config/defaults.json overrides two keys vs BUILTIN_DEFAULTS: doc.catalogPath='docs/catalog.json' (not the .ts) and doc.docsIndexRoute='/prds' (not '/docs'). It stamps engineVersion='0.1.0' and echoes all other keys identically. As source 2 (above BUILTIN_DEFAULTS source 1), the bundled file wins these two keys for any repo without a per-repo override. [code: config/defaults.json:1-15; src/config.mjs:84]
hermes_relevance: After `htw init` (which writes the defaults.json-matching starter), the catalog is at docs/catalog.json and the back-link points to /prds. Hermes uses these paths when navigating the output.

user_story: As a project author, I want to set the brand name shown in rendered docs so the doc site reflects my project name rather than the generic 'Docs' label.
expected_behavior: config.brandName is a top-level string; default 'Docs' in both BUILTIN_DEFAULTS and defaults.json. `htw init` sets it to basename(root) (the consuming repo's directory name). The engine reads it; a per-repo override in the canonical config wins. [code: src/config.mjs:37; src/commands/init.mjs:91]
hermes_relevance: Hermes sets brandName when bootstrapping a project. Pure display label — changing it never affects routing, paths, or API behavior.

user_story: As a project author, I want to keep repo-local doc templates and theme files in a predictable directory so the engine finds them without per-file config.
expected_behavior: doc.overlayDir is the relative dir holding repo-local overlay files such as templates/theme.css; default '.agents/skill-config/doc'. The engine checks for a repo overlay theme at join(overlayDir,'templates/theme.css') during theme resolution (tiers 3 of the cascade). Changing this key relocates all overlay lookups. [code: src/config.mjs:39; src/doc-kit.mjs:59,89]
hermes_relevance: Hermes need not change overlayDir — the default is the canonical agents home. A custom theme.css must go at {overlayDir}/templates/theme.css relative to repo root.

user_story: As a brand author, I want to supply my own complete CSS that fully replaces the engine's bundled theme so my site has its own identity with no inheritance from defaults.
expected_behavior: When doc.themeFile is a non-null/non-empty path (absolute or repo-relative), resolveThemePath() returns it directly and resolveThemeTokensPath() returns null (themeFile wins; themeTokens ignored). themeCss() reads the file verbatim and inlines it into every page. This is the highest-precedence theme override. A missing file does NOT fall back gracefully — readText() throws; the path must exist. [code: src/doc-kit.mjs:77-92,98-102,107-111]

user_story: As a project author who wants a different palette but no custom components, I want to write a ~15-line :root{} block that re-skins the whole surface without touching the engine.
expected_behavior: When doc.themeFile is null/absent and doc.themeTokens points to an existing file, resolveThemeTokensPath() resolves it (isAbsolute check + existsSync; missing => returns null silently, never crashes a render). themeCss() concatenates base theme + separator comment + patch CSS. The patch's :root, appended after base, wins the cascade and overrides any base token. The patch may add @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark). Ignored when themeFile is set (tier-1 wins). [code: src/doc-kit.mjs:94-111]
hermes_relevance: Lightweight theming for agent-configured repos: write a small :root{} patch, set the key, invoke render. A missing patch file is safe (silent fallback to base). The patch is CSS, not JSON — Hermes must write valid CSS; invalid CSS is inlined verbatim and may produce broken styling.

feature: Three-tier theme resolution cascade + repo-overlay discovery
user_story: As an engine consumer, I want a clear priority order for theme CSS so I know exactly which file wins when multiple theme sources exist, including a repo overlay.
expected_behavior: resolveThemePath() precedence: (1) doc.themeFile set => return it (themeTokens ignored); (2) overlayTheme = join(overlayDir,'templates/theme.css') under ROOT, if existsSync => return it; (3) bundled join(PACKAGE_ROOT,'templates/theme.css'). For tier-2 token patches the BASE is the overlay theme if present, else the package theme — never the repo root's random files. A repo overlay (e.g. Homebase's .agents/skill-config/doc/templates/theme.css) is auto-discovered and preferred over the bundled default with no config key. Read at render time, so changes take effect on the next render. [code: src/doc-kit.mjs:75-111; src/config.mjs:39]
hermes_relevance: Agents in a repo with an overlay theme need no config change — the engine finds it automatically (this kept Homebase's theme byte-identical after extraction). A fresh repo (htw init) lands at the bundled theme unless files are explicitly placed.

user_story: As a doc author running htw register, I want the catalog stored in the right format for my repo type (JSON array for generic repos, TypeScript for Homebase) so register/verify work without configuration.
expected_behavior: doc.catalogPath is the catalog path. BUILTIN_DEFAULTS='src/modules/docs/catalog.ts'; defaults.json (and htw init's starter)='docs/catalog.json'. When it ends in .json, register writes a JSON-array catalog and verify parses by id/href. When .ts, the engine does an in-place TS splice. If the .ts path does not exist on a non-Homebase repo, register warns and skips instead of crashing. [code: src/doc-kit.mjs:1829-1842; src/commands/init.mjs:97]
hermes_relevance: On a freshly initialized repo, register produces a JSON-array catalog at docs/catalog.json — strictly better for Hermes than the TS splice; it can read/parse it directly to enumerate registered docs.

user_story: As an agent authoring docs, I want htw commands to find my .doc.md sources in the configured directory so I do not specify paths on every command.
expected_behavior: doc.sourcesDir (relative to root) is where .doc.md sources live; default 'docs/sources'. Used by render/register/verify --all when resolving sources. Changing it relocates the --all glob search. [code: src/config.mjs:42; src/doc-kit.mjs:61]
hermes_relevance: Hermes writes new .doc.md files here and can pass --all knowing they're found. For machine-driven flows, explicit paths are more reliable than --all when Hermes knows which docs changed.

user_story: As an agent rendering docs, I want rendered PRD HTML in a predictable directory so I can construct the doc URL and serve it without guessing.
expected_behavior: doc.prdsDir (relative) is where PRD HTML is written; default 'docs/prds'. A PRD slug 'my-prd' outputs to {prdsDir}/my-prd/index.html with route /{prdsDir}/my-prd/. Also used by `htw grill` to locate the rendered HTML when parsing question IDs. [code: src/config.mjs:43; src/doc-kit.mjs:62,1282; src/commands/grill.mjs:179]
hermes_relevance: After render, Hermes predicts {prdsDir}/{slug}/index.html and serve URL /{prdsDir}/{slug}/. grill ask reads prdsDir to find the rendered doc and extract question IDs — Hermes must render before grill ask.

expected_behavior: doc.plansDir (relative) is where report/working-doc HTML is written; default 'docs/plans'. A plan slug 'my-plan' outputs to {plansDir}/my-plan.html (flat file, not a subdirectory) with route /{plansDir}/my-plan.html. [code: src/config.mjs:44; src/doc-kit.mjs:63,1277,1283]

hermes_relevance: Hermes sets docsIndexRoute to match whatever route the app mounts the doc index at. Purely a navigation aid — does not affect any CLI behavior or output structure.

[code: src/config.mjs:46; src/doc-kit.mjs:48-72]
hermes_relevance: Hermes driving a non-standard lifecycle (e.g. an Ops team vocabulary) sets stagesPath. Most repos leave it null and inherit the engine's default 6 stages.

When relative (no http:// prefix), grill.mjs prepends config.devUrlBase (default http://127.0.0.1:8765) to form an absolute URL. Routes appended as {base}/open, {base}/result, {base}/close. Overridable per-invocation with --base. [code: src/config.mjs:48; src/commands/grill.mjs:64-72]

'local' = repo runs `htw serve --answer-gate`, gate live on localhost. 'custom' = consuming host provides an onAnswer callback. `htw check` validates mode is one of the three and exits 1 on any other value. [code: src/commands/check.mjs:58,141-152; src/commands/init.mjs:107]
